reasoning: B and A both nail the descriptive/prescriptive split, the ceteris-paribus suppressed premise, OVB/extrapolation, and the sequential-game/Lucas-critique structure. B edges ahead on calibration by giving explicit, defensible probability ranges (60-70% conditional vs 25-35% unconditional; 40-60% incumbent match) AND by steelmanning the second advisor before flagging that both advisors committed the same one-branch error, plus pre-committed kill criteria and a premortem against cash runway. A is nearly as strong with the cleanest payoff-matrix reasoning and the sharpest 'a model sound enough to destroy the world it forecasts' framing, but quantifies uncertainty less. C uniquely surfaces the revenue-vs-contribution denominator (the 17.6%/43%/100% volume-hurdle arithmetic), a concrete insight A/B/D omit, but it is less calibrated (no probabilities) and weaker on game-theoretic structure. D is solid and explicitly names the Lucas critique and a p/(1-p) fork with magnitudes, but adds less beyond the shared core than A or B.
